mnemonics: D.Q. D.A.M.A. (Data Quality Dimensions: Accuracy, Completeness, Consistency, Uniqueness, Timeliness, Validity - often remembered with the acronym 'ACCURATE' but D.A.M.A. is a key institute)
keyPoints: A Data Quality Initiative is a formal program to measure, improve, and sustain the quality of data in an organization.
It involves defining data quality dimensions, establishing metrics, continuous monitoring, and implementing improvement processes.
Successful initiatives require executive sponsorship, cross-functional teamwork, and integration into standard business processes.
commonMistakes: Treating data quality as a one-time project instead of an ongoing, operational initiative.
Focusing only on technical data cleansing without addressing the underlying business processes that create the poor-quality data.
Failing to establish clear ownership and accountability for data quality across the organization.
